content.text:
Now I have a clear view of the molecule with atom labels visible. Let me examine atom C:15 specifically to identify all its covalent neighbors.

From the image, I can see atom C:15 (carbon, orange/gold sphere) is connected to:
- N:14 (nitrogen, blue sphere) - bonded to C:15
- C:16 (carbon, orange/gold sphere) - bonded to C:15  
- H:18 (hydrogen, white/gray sphere) - bonded to C:15

Looking at the connections more carefully, C:15 appears to have exactly three covalent bonds: one to N:14, one to C:16, and one to H:18.

The geomconf_index can be determined from the filename "geomconf_8830.xyz", which indicates geomconf_index = 8830.

Therefore, the neighbor atoms of atom C:15 are:
- Atomic index 14, element N (nitrogen)
- Atomic index 16, element C (carbon)
- Atomic index 18, element H (hydrogen)

Geomconf_index: 8830

question: Please find the neighbour atoms of the atom with atomic index 15, whose element should be C. Here, neighbour atoms are defined as atoms that are covalently connected to this atom. Report all neighbour atoms by listing their atomic index (as shown in the image) and their element. Also return the geomconf_index for this molecule.
